mnemonics: Very Fast Offspring: VFORK creates a child process that runs Very Fast in the parent's Own (address) space until it execs or exits, preventing costly copies.
keyPoints: The child process from vfork() shares the parent's memory address space, unlike fork().
The parent is suspended until the child process calls either exec() or _exit().
Modifying data in the vfork() child (except for the variable used to store the return value of vfork) before an exec() leads to undefined behavior.
commonMistakes: Assuming the child process has its own copy of the memory space and modifying variables, which corrupts the parent's data.
Letting the child process return from the function that called vfork() instead of using _exit(), causing a crash.
